title: sarothamnus scoparius var. andreanus
intro: Sarothamnus scoparius var. andreanus, commonly known as broom, is a flowering plant that belongs to the pea family (Fabaceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of various respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits include its ability to relieve coughs, soothe sore throats, and act as a natural diuretic. The herbal preparations of broom typically involve infusions, tinctures, and teas, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
